coordinate_system: CHM13 v2.0 chromosome coordinates; BED columns are 0-based half-open
renderer_notes: Main interval track for CHM13-style panels. Use CHM13-HG002.sub-telo-phrs.bed when comparing CHM13 with HG002 PanSN names.

coordinate_system: HPRCv2 1Mb trimmed PanSN sequence coordinates; region_start and region_end are 0-based half-open offsets within seq
draw_as: Filled interval nested within the 1Mb sequence window
renderer_notes: Canonical row-level projected PHR table for cohort/haplotype panels. Convert relative offsets to chromosome coordinates only when seq encodes a simple CHM13 interval and the renderer explicitly needs absolute coordinates.
Use these C1-C15 labels for this talk. Do not use sequence-level community IDs as if they were arm-level C1-C15.

coordinate_system: GFF3 is 1-based closed; phrs.genes.gff3 is already intersected to CHM13 PHR intervals
draw_as: Directional gene glyphs with compact labels
renderer_notes: Use phrs.genes.gff3 for CHM13 target panels. Use per-haplotype HPRC GFF3 only for cohort-specific tracks.

coordinate_system: Community and arm summary tables; no per-feature genomic coordinates
draw_as: Small badges or colored gene-label classes
renderer_notes: Use these tables to group labels such as DUX4L, OR4F, IL9R/IL9RP, RPL23A, SEPTIN14, DDX11L, WASH, MTCO. They should not replace the gene coordinates.

coordinate_system: RepeatMasker BED is 0-based half-open; RDS contains clipped TAR1 entries relative to PHR regions
renderer_notes: TAR1 should be drawn as a repeat block. For D4Z4, no standalone BED was found in the reviewed outputs; draw DUX4L/DUX4 gene array plus d4z4_perwindow_signal.tsv as the D4Z4 proxy unless a D4Z4 repeat BED is supplied.

coordinate_system: Summary tables by arm/community; mixed coordinate conventions
draw_as: Side labels or small inset metrics
renderer_notes: Use for concise numeric callouts. Keep them outside the main coordinate track so the browser view remains readable.
Use to explain why a locus belongs in the talk. State that community-family Fisher rows are qualitative context when significant is FALSE.
